pe:description: Codified rules for classifying raw Figma get_variable_defs output into DS-ONT three-tier cascade (Primitive → Semantic → Component). Applied during PE-DS-EXTRACT-001 Phase 2.

classificationStrategy.description: Classification uses two signals: (1) which Figma section/frame the token was extracted from determines the default tier, (2) the variable name pattern confirms or overrides the section-based classification.

sectionToTierMap.description: Maps Figma section names (from get_metadata) to default DS-ONT tier and semantic group. Section name matching is case-insensitive, prefix-based.

rules.description: Top-level colour section contains both semantic subsections and primitive colour scales. Classify children by their own section pattern match.

variableNameToTokenName.description: Maps Figma variable slash-path names to DS-ONT ds:tokenName dot-path format. Applied after section-based tier assignment.

valueTypeInference.description: Infer ds:valueType from the token value format when not explicitly mapped.

rules.note: Numeric-only values (4, 8, 12, 24) — context determines Spacing vs Radius

excludePatterns.description: Variable names matching these patterns are excluded from classification (noise/duplicates).

patterns.reason: Appears in every section as a size reference — duplicate of spacing.lg primitive
Duplicate of Surface/ tokens in some Figma files — deduplicate by preferring Surface/ naming
Figma SDS system variables — map to explicit token names instead of preserving var() references
Warning-specific border strokes — already captured via Warning section Border/ tokens

componentTierRules.description: Component-tier tokens are not directly extractable from Figma get_variable_defs — they are authored via the DesignComponent editor in the visualiser (S7.6.4). Classification rule: any token with a component-scoped name (button.*, input.*, card.*, checkbox.*) is Component tier.
componentTierRules.note: Component tokens reference semantic tokens — BR-DS-002 enforces this cascade integrity.

themeModeMapping.description: Rules for mapping extracted tokens to DS-ONT ThemeMode entities (light/dark). Most Figma brand files define only light mode values — dark mode is derived or pending.

detectionStrategy.step1: Check if Figma file has separate 'Dark Mode' or 'Dark' section in get_metadata output.
detectionStrategy.step2: If yes: extract dark mode section tokens and populate ds:darkModeValue on SemanticTokens.
detectionStrategy.step3: If no (typical): set darkModeStatus='derived' and apply derivation rules below.
detectionStrategy.step4: Generate ThemeMode entities: ds:mode-light (always), ds:mode-dark (if dark values present or derived).

darkModeDerivationRules.description: When Figma has no dark mode section, dark mode values can be derived from the primitive colour scale by inverting the scale position. These are approximate — design review recommended.

rules.derivation: Use 900-step value from the same colour scale
Use 700-step value from the same colour scale
Use 300-step value from the same colour scale
Invert luminance — dark text on light bg becomes light text on dark bg
Use near-black value from neutral scale (DR-CANVAS-001: luminance < 0.05)

darkModeStatusValues.meaning: Dark mode values extracted directly from a Figma dark mode section
Dark mode values computed from derivation rules — approximate, needs design review
Dark mode values exist in CSS but Figma dark section not yet created
Brand does not support dark mode (ds:themeModeSupport=false)

expectedYields.description: Expected token counts per tier after classification and deduplication, based on verified BAIV extraction.

component.note: Zero is valid for brands without component tokens — authored separately via S7.6.4
